
Reviewed Grok social signals and arXiv papers. Most Grok signals either duplicate existing README entries (XML prompting, Projects, computer use, scratchpads) or point to unverifiable/fabricated URLs with placeholder tweet IDs. One signal — Claude Artifacts for rapid prototyping — is distinct, actionable, and not yet in the README, with a plausible primary source. The arXiv papers are all generic AI/agent research with no Claude-specific practical tips. Proposing only 1 high-confidence addition.

## Suggestions

- [Claude Artifacts Workflow for Rapid Prototyping](https://www.anthropic.com/news/artifacts) — Anthropic's documented Artifacts feature enables real-time iterative editing loops for code, documents, and mini-apps directly in Claude — a non-obvious workflow that significantly accelerates prototyping cycles week over week.
  Section: Tips of the Week | Criteria: Practical, actionable tip or technique for using Claude more effectively, Grounded in real usage evidence (Anthropic official release post), Applicable to a broad audience (developers, writers, analysts), Novel or non-obvious — Artifacts' iterative editing loop is distinct from basic chat usage and not covered in existing README entries
